commands.review_summary:
The PR adds safe record/array boundary helpers and applies them to provider catalog, model-row, paired-provider, and provider-auth projection paths with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +124, Tests +159. Total +283 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main uses direct object/array/property projection on provider catalog and auth rows, and the branch's throwing-getter tests demonstrate the failure path without needing live credentials.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This PR is not an automation repair candidate because the remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of compatibility/auth-provider semantics, not a narrow code defect.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): isolate provider catalog projection failures
target.body:
## Summary

- isolates provider catalog projection failures so unreadable provider, model, or auth rows do not drop healthy sibling entries
- adds shared safe record/array boundary helpers for plugin-owned provider metadata
- keeps the change scoped to provider catalog/auth/model projection paths and their focused regressions
- uses only synthetic provider/plugin fixtures in tests and PR notes

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 1 unit-fast shard, 14 tests passed on final rebased head `7bf6bd0571`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.ts src/shared/safe-record.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- touched-file scrub for reported plugin/tool names, private paths, and private network literals: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean after final rebase, no accepted/actionable findings
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: AWS Crabbox run `run_cb7d38037c6f`, lease `cbx_f60c3a25aaf6`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`, exit 0

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed provider catalog/auth/model metadata no longer crashes provider catalog projection or hides healthy sibling provider entries.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree rebased onto `origin/main` `9caefeaf08c`, plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate after rebasing the provider slice onto current main at the time of the run.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: cherry-picked the narrow provider-catalog slice onto current main, ran the focused provider-entry/provider-catalog tests, formatter check, diff check, touched-file scrub, autoreview, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`. After `main` moved once more, rebased again and reran focused sanity plus final autoreview.

Evidence after fix: tests prove unreadable provider catalog entries, catalog model rows, and auth option rows are skipped while healthy sibling entries remain available.

Observed result after fix: one malformed plugin-owned provider metadata row no longer poisons the whole provider catalog surface.

What was not tested: no full live provider setup flow; behavior is covered by provider SDK/catalog unit tests and the remote changed gate including extension typecheck lanes.
target.author: vincentkoc
